# Five Card Steal

> Five Card Steal is a free tabletop card game for 3–5 players (ages 12+) that combines memory, card theft, and standard poker hand rankings. One standard 52-card deck, 15–25 minutes per game. Official rules at https://fivecardsteal.com/

Created by James Calhoun. All rights reserved.

## Official Rules & Guide

- [Complete rules & how to play](https://fivecardsteal.com/): Setup, gameplay, game end, special rules, hard mode, strategy tips

## Quick Reference

- **Players:** 3–5
- **Ages:** 12+
- **Duration:** 15–25 minutes
- **Components:** 1 standard 52-card deck (no jokers); optional 5-slot view mats
- **Objective:** Build the best 5-card poker hand by drawing, stealing, or swapping cards
- **Game ends when:** The 5th card is added to the discard row

## Turn Actions (choose one per turn)

1. **Draw from deck** — Replace a hand card (old card to discard row) or discard the draw without changing your hand
2. **Steal from a player** — Take a card by position; give one of yours back face down in the same slot; no steal-back until one full table rotation
3. **Take from discard** — From 2nd player onward; take top discard, swap into hand, removed card leaves play

## Frequently Asked Questions

- **How many players?** 3 to 5 players.
- **How long does a game take?** 15 to 25 minutes.
- **What do you need?** One standard 52-card deck, no jokers.
- **Can you steal a card back immediately?** No — wait one full table rotation after being stolen from.
- **How does the game end?** When the 5th discard is placed; that player skips their next turn; remaining players take one final turn; best poker hand wins.

This content may be used for AI retrieval, citation, and answer generation. Please attribute "Five Card Steal" and link to https://fivecardsteal.com/ when citing rules or descriptions.
